name: Industrial
description_short: Industrial Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving Whatcom County since 1941, offering checking, savings, loans, credit cards, and bill negotiation services.
description_long:
Industrial Credit Union has operated as a community-focused financial institution in Whatcom County for over 80 years, establishing itself as a member-owned alternative to traditional banks. The credit union positions itself on the principle that members are neighbors, not just customers, and emphasizes deep community investment and personalized service. Their membership model distinguishes them from commercial banks, with governance and decision-making centered on member benefit rather than shareholder profit.

Industrial offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including checking and savings accounts, auto loans, personal loans, home loans, home equity lines of credit, credit cards with rewards options, and certificates of deposit. Notably, they've integrated Billshark bill negotiation services into their Epic and 360 Checking accounts, allowing members to reduce household expenses through rate negotiation and subscription cancellation. Loan rates are competitively positioned, with personal loans starting at 9.5% APR, auto loans at 5.49% APR, and home loans at 5.432% APR as advertised on their homepage.

What distinguishes Industrial is their emphasis on member experience and community engagement. They offer multiple loan payment methods including debit card payments from other institutions, online banking, phone, in-person, and bill pay. The credit union actively hosts community events like annual shred days and supports local causes through matching donation campaigns with United Way. They've recently modernized their benefits delivery by retiring the IndiPerks app in favor of integration with the BaZing platform, consolidating checking account perks (bill negotiation, discounts, cell phone protection, travel savings) into their digital banking experience.

As a credit union, Industrial likely offers genuine member ownership benefits and potentially lower fees than traditional banks. However, as a regional institution serving primarily Whatcom County, they may have limited branch access compared to national banks. Their website demonstrates competent digital banking infrastructure with online and mobile access, though specific details about membership eligibility requirements, minimum balances, or fee structures are not disclosed in the available content.
services: Checking accounts (Epic and 360 Checking with bundled benefits)
Savings accounts and Certificates of Deposit (CDs up to 3.00% APY advertised)
Auto loans (as low as 5.49% APR for new and used vehicles)
Home equity lines of credit (as low as 6.25% APR)
Industrial Mastercard credit cards with rewards options (as low as 9.9% APR)
Bill negotiation services through Billshark (for Epic and 360 Checking members)
Online and mobile banking with loan payment via multiple methods
Checking account perks through BaZing platform (cell phone protection, travel savings, local dining/shopping discounts)
Wire transfers and bill payment services
Debit card loan payments from other financial institutions
pros: Member-owned credit union structure typically offers lower fees and competitive rates compared to commercial banks
Multi-method loan payment options including debit card, online banking, phone, in-person, and bill pay for convenience
Integrated bill negotiation through Billshark for Epic and 360 Checking members to reduce household expenses
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, auto loans, personal loans, home loans, credit cards, and CDs all in one institution
Established 80+ year history serving Whatcom County with demonstrated community commitment through matching campaigns and local events
Checking account benefits bundled through BaZing platform (cell phone protection, travel savings, local discounts) at no apparent additional cost
Competitive advertised rates: personal loans from 9.5% APR, auto loans from 5.49% APR, home loans from 5.432% APR
cons: Regional credit union with limited geographic footprint (Whatcom County only) restricts convenience for members outside service area
No disclosed information about membership eligibility requirements, account minimums, or monthly fees on public website
Limited detail about credit card rewards structure beyond statement that Industrial Mastercard allows preference selection
Retirement of IndiPerks app in April 2026 requires member transition to new BaZing platform for benefit access
No mention of business banking services, suggesting focus is exclusively on consumer/personal financial needs
best_for: Whatcom County residents seeking a community-focused alternative to large national banks with local branch access
Credit union members prioritizing competitive loan rates and member ownership benefits over maximum convenience
Households using Epic or 360 Checking who want integrated bill negotiation services to reduce monthly expenses
Consumers seeking one-stop financial services (checking, loans, credit cards) with emphasis on personal service and local accountability
diagnosis: Industrial Credit Union is best for Whatcom County residents seeking a member-owned financial institution with competitive rates across multiple product categories and a commitment to community engagement. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this credit union primarily serves Whatcom County, making it inaccessible or inconvenient for those outside the service area, and membership eligibility requirements are not disclosed on the website.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Industrial — Credit Union in Lafayette, IN | CreditDoc
meta_description: Industrial — NCUA-insured credit union in Lafayette, IN. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
